learning_outcomes.description: Articulate relevant theoretical frameworks for group and community work, and recognise different forms of community, and the implications for inclusive social work practice.
Demonstrate how social work values inform group and community practice and contribute to culturally responsive and inclusive approaches with diverse communities.
Apply research skills to access and interpret data on community needs and capacities and use this evidence to inform effective community consultation and practice decisions.
Apply foundational social work skills in engaging, assessing and intervening with groups and communities, including the use of community education processes.
Use community consultation skills and community work methods to contribute to influencing and implementing social policy.

assessments.assessment_title: Community Work Practice Skills and Reflection
assessments.due_date: 21/8/2026 - 11/9/2026 [Part A & Part B: Week 4, Week 5, Week 6 & Week 7]
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
This description is a brief summary of the assessment task. For full details, including the marking rubric, please refer to the course Learn UQ (Blackboard Ultra) site.

The assessment consists of two parts.

Part A: Group Workshop [Group Task - Secure]

Weighting: 30% 

Description:

In the four tutorials from Week 4 (21 August) to Week 7 (11 September), students will work in groups (4 students in each group) to develop and then lead/facilitate one of four community work practice skills workshops. Students who are not leading the weekly workshops will attend as participants and reflect on the workshop. The skills workshops are an opportunity for students to develop and apply group work and community work practice skills.

Group Allocations: Your tutor will organise group membership, topics and the workshop schedule in your first tutorial of the semester (Week 2). Please ensure that you attend this class. If you are unable to attend, you will be placed in a group and provided the contact details of your group members. It is your responsibility to make contact with your group as soon as possible upon receipt of these details. 

Tutorial Participation Requirement: Your participation in the skills workshops in Week 4, Week 5, Week 6 and Week 7 is necessary to meet the learning outcomes required by Australian Association of Social Workers (AASW) accreditation standards. Therefore, your tutor will be taking attendance in these weeks. If a member of the team is ill, then remainder of the team will facilitate their workshop and indicate which section the absent student was to facilitate. The student who was absent will then facilitate that section of the workshop in their tutorial group in a subsequent tutorial timeslot.

Part B: Individual Reflective Journaling Activity (completed and submitted each week) [Individual - Secure]

Weighting: 20%

Description:

For this assessment, each student will write 4 journal entries in class time, one for each of the four workshops in Week 4, Week 5, Week 6 and Week 7. In each entry, students will write their response to a reflective prompt provided in each workshop to be completed in class time. Each entry will be written on a separate Task Sheet and submitted to your course tutor at the end of the writing period. 

If a student is unable to attend and participate in the community work skills tutorials due to illness or an unexpected personal crisis, they will have the opportunity to submit a catch-up reflective journal entry (Part B) on the same topic as the workshop missed. This will be scheduled by your tutor in consultation with the course coordinator. Students will be required to present a medical certificate or evidence of an unanticipated personal crisis that prevented their participation in the tutorial on that day to remain eligible to submit a journal entry for a missed workshop. Please note that if you do not have the required supporting evidence, you will not be offered an opportunity to make up the reflection task and are thus not eligible for the 5 marks for that week’s reflection.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This description is a brief summary of the task. For full details, including the marking rubric, please refer to the course Learn UQ (Blackboard Ultra) site.

Description:

The aim of the Community Development Project is for students to demonstrate the capacity to design a community project that addresses community need and builds capacity underpinned by community development principles and skills. The project will also meet the course learning objective to demonstrate culturally responsive practice approaches to working with groups and communities. 

Students will have the opportunity to design a community development project in the form of a funding application. The funding proposal is to be completed on a pre-formatted document. Please adhere to the word counts in each section of your funding application.

Project Planning

You are expected to engage in a project planning process to develop your community development projects in the tutorials. Tutorial worksheet and activities are designed to support this process. Evidence of project planning is required for this assessment (please refer to the Community Development Project Marking Rubric in the course site). Please ensure that you participate in at least one project planning tutorial. These tutorials are in Week 8, Week 9 and Week 10. Your tutor will record your participation as evidence of this requirement. Should you be unable to attend and participate in a project planning tutorial due to ill health or unanticipated personal crisis, then a catch-up worksheet must be completed and submitted to the Tutor to ensure that you have met this course learning outcome. If a student is on a regional placement, then an online project planning tutorial will be organised.   

Use of AI: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. While students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment requires students to submit an electronic version of their assignment via Blackboard Ultra, using the Turnitin software. Please refer to ITS help if you experience difficulties in submitting your assessment in Blackboard or using Turnitin.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Overview and Introduction to the course

In this introductory session, we explore group work and community development as methods of social work practice. We examine community as the focus and context of social work practice. Through the course overview, you'll gain an understanding of the course structure, expectations and requirements which will enable you to engage meaningfully with the learning over the next 13 weeks.

Please note: There are no tutorials this week.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Overview and Introduction to the course In this introductory session, we explore group work and community development as methods of social work practice. We examine community as the focus and context of social work practice. Through the course overview, you'll gain an understanding of the course structure, expectations and requirements which will enable you to engage meaningfully with the learning over the next 13 weeks. Please note: There are no tutorials this week.

learning_activities.topic:
Community Education

Today we focus on the theory and practice of community education. This class will provide the foundation for planning and facilitating your group workshops in Weeks 4-7 of the course.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Community Education Today we focus on the theory and practice of community education. This class will provide the foundation for planning and facilitating your group workshops in Weeks 4-7 of the course.

learning_activities.topic:
Introductory Tutorial

In this first tutorial, we will introduce ourselves and set group agreements for how we will work together over the semester. We will organise group membership, topics and the schedule for the group workshops and begin to consider community work practice skills.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Introductory Tutorial In this first tutorial, we will introduce ourselves and set group agreements for how we will work together over the semester. We will organise group membership, topics and the schedule for the group workshops and begin to consider community work practice skills.

learning_activities.topic:
Culturally Responsive Community Work

Today we host a guest practitioner who will guide our learning about protocols for respectful engagement with culturally diverse communities.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Culturally Responsive Community Work Today we host a guest practitioner who will guide our learning about protocols for respectful engagement with culturally diverse communities.

learning_activities.topic:
Community Education

We review community education principles and identify important practice skills for the group workshops. We will undertake reflective activities to prepare for writing workshop reflections. We will discuss approaches to, and skills in, engaging with culturally diverse communities.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Community Education We review community education principles and identify important practice skills for the group workshops. We will undertake reflective activities to prepare for writing workshop reflections. We will discuss approaches to, and skills in, engaging with culturally diverse communities.

learning_activities.topic:
Working with Groups in a Community Context

We discuss the types of groups in a community work context. We explore the different ways community workers facilitate group participation. This theme will continue in the tutorial.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Working with Groups in a Community Context
learning_activities.topic_description: Working with Groups in a Community Context We discuss the types of groups in a community work context. We explore the different ways community workers facilitate group participation. This theme will continue in the tutorial.

learning_activities.topic:
Maximising Group Participation

This tutorial begins with the first skills workshop which focuses on achieving inclusive group leadership through maximising participation in groups. Students will engage in the first reflective journaling activity. We will discuss material from the course readings with a focus on the stage theory of groups.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Maximising Group Participation This tutorial begins with the first skills workshop which focuses on achieving inclusive group leadership through maximising participation in groups. Students will engage in the first reflective journaling activity.  We will discuss material from the course readings with a focus on the stage theory of groups.

learning_activities.topic:
Groups as Vehicles for Change

We return to the theme of working with groups and consider some practical examples of how community groups are being used to achieve positive social change.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Groups as Vehicles for Change We return to the theme of working with groups and consider some practical examples of how community groups are being used to achieve positive social change.

learning_activities.topic:
Managing Conflict in Groups

The second student skills workshop addresses the topic of understanding and managing conflict in groups. We discuss conflict as an inevitable part of groups and consider how conflict can be managed constructively. Students will engage in the second reflective journaling activity. 

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Managing Conflict in Groups The second student skills workshop addresses the topic of understanding and managing conflict in groups. We discuss conflict as an inevitable part of groups and consider how conflict can be managed constructively. Students will engage in the second reflective journaling activity.

learning_activities.topic:
Asset Based Community Development

We explore the principles of an Asset Based Approach to Community Development.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Asset Based Community Development We explore the principles of an Asset Based Approach to Community Development.

learning_activities.topic:
Assessing Community Needs & Capacities

Assessing a community's needs and capacities is the topic of today's group workshop. We will discuss the Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) approach. Students will engage in their third reflective journaling activity.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Assessing Community Needs & Capacities Assessing a community's needs and capacities is the topic of today's group workshop. We will discuss the Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) approach. Students will engage in their third reflective journaling activity.

learning_activities.topic:
A Critical Practice Perspective

We consider critical forms of community work that are aimed at creating positive social change.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: A Critical Practice Perspective We consider critical forms of community work that are aimed at creating positive social change.

learning_activities.topic:
Working with the Media

The final skills workshop addresses working with the media (print or social media) to represent and advocate for your community. Students learn the skill of preparing media releases in community practice. We consider whether generative AI can play a role. Students will engage in their final reflective journaling activity.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Working with the Media The final skills workshop addresses working with the media (print or social media) to represent and advocate for your community. Students learn the skill of preparing media releases in community practice. We consider whether generative AI can play a role. Students will engage in their final reflective journaling activity.

learning_activities.topic:
Community Consultation and Advocacy

Today we explore the social worker's role in representing and advocating for/with communities through community consultative processes.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Community Consultation and Advocacy Today we explore the social worker's role in representing and advocating for/with communities through community consultative processes.

learning_activities.topic:
Community Consultation Processes

Project Planning: In this tutorial, we commence the process of planning your community development project. A tutorial worksheet and activities are designed to support this process.

Note: You are expected to engage in the project planning process. Evidence of project planning is required for Assessment Two (please refer to the Community Development Project Marking Rubric in the course site). Your tutor will record your participation as evidence of this requirement.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Community Consultation Processes Project Planning: In this tutorial, we commence the process of planning your community development project. A tutorial worksheet and activities are designed to support this process. Note: You are expected to engage in the project planning process. Evidence of project planning is required for Assessment Two (please refer to the Community Development Project Marking Rubric in the course site ) . Your tutor will record your participation as evidence of this requirement.

learning_activities.topic:
Community Work Practice & Policy Processes

We continue to explore community consultation processes from a practice perspective. We focus on the social work role and writing an advocacy paper.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Community Work Practice & Policy Processes We continue to explore community consultation processes from a practice perspective. We focus on the social work role and writing an advocacy paper.

learning_activities.topic:
Community Consultation Processes

Project Planning: In this tutorial, we continue to explore community consultation processes and work together to discuss and plan your community development project.

Note: You are expected to engage in the project planning process. Evidence of project planning is required for Assessment Two (please refer to the Community Development Project Marking Rubric in the course site). Your tutor will record your participation as evidence of this requirement.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05

Note: You are expected to engage in the project planning process. Evidence of project planning is required for Assessment Two (please refer to the Community Development Project Marking Rubric in the course site ) . Your tutor will record your participation as evidence of this requirement.

learning_activities.topic:
Preparing your Funding Proposal Part 1

We consider how social workers in community practice access funds for community activities. We discuss the following elements of your funding submission for your community development project: writing a budget, timeline and milestones.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Preparing your Funding Proposal Part 1 We consider how social workers in community practice access funds for community activities. We discuss the following elements of your funding submission for your community development project: writing a budget, timeline and milestones.

Preparing your Funding Proposal Part 1

In this tutorial, we conclude the project planning phase. We will focus on the skills involved in writing a funding submission. The class will engage in writing exercises. 

Note: You are expected to engage in the project planning process. Evidence of project planning is required for Assessment Two (please refer to the Community Development Project Marking Rubric in the course site). Your tutor will record your participation as evidence of this requirement.

learning_activities.topic:
Preparing your Funding Proposal Part 2

We continue to focus on the funding submission for your community development project and consider all the components. We discuss the community development principles and values underpinning your project design.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Preparing your Funding Proposal Part 2
